questions.q: Who headed the Sachar Committee established in 2005?
questions.answer: Justice Rajindar Sachar headed the Sachar Committee.

questions.q: What is the main objective of the National Counter Terrorism Centre (NCTC)?
questions.answer: The main objective of the National Counter Terrorism Centre (NCTC) is to coordinate anti-terrorism activities across the country.

questions.q: What is the significance of the 73rd and 74th Amendments to the Indian Constitution?
questions.answer: These amendments provide for the establishment of three-tier Panchayati Raj Institutions at the village, intermediate, and district levels, and also for the establishment of Municipalities at the urban level.

questions.q: Who is the head of the National Security Council (NSC)?
questions.answer: The Prime Minister of India is the head of the National Security Council (NSC).

questions.q: What is the significance of the National Policy on Education (NPE) 1986?
questions.answer: The National Policy on Education (NPE) 1986 aimed at providing free and compulsory education to all children up to the age of 14.

questions.q: What is the role of the Planning Commission in the Indian economy?
questions.answer: The Planning Commission played a key role in formulating the Five-Year Plans and providing overall direction to the Indian economy.

questions.q: What is the significance of the 42nd Amendment to the Indian Constitution?
questions.answer: The 42nd Amendment to the Indian Constitution strengthened the federal structure and gave more power to the states.

questions.q: What is the main objective of the Right to Information (RTI) Act, 2005?
questions.answer: The main objective of the Right to Information (RTI) Act, 2005 is to provide citizens with the right to access information held by public authorities.

questions.q: What is the significance of the National Human Rights Commission (NHRC)?
questions.answer: The National Human Rights Commission (NHRC) is a constitutional body that protects and promotes human rights in India.

questions.q: What is the main objective of the National Commission to Review the Working of the Constitution (NCRWC)?
questions.answer: The main objective of the National Commission to Review the Working of the Constitution (NCRWC) was to review the working of the Constitution and recommend necessary changes.

questions.q: What is the significance of the National Environment Policy (NEP) 2006?
questions.answer: The National Environment Policy (NEP) 2006 aimed at promoting sustainable development and protecting the environment in India.
Inclusive growth refers to the process of economic growth that benefits all sections of society, particularly the vulnerable and disadvantaged groups.

questions.q: What are the main features of the 73rd and 74th Amendments to the Indian Constitution?
questions.answer: The 73rd and 74th Amendments provide for the establishment of three-tier Panchayati Raj Institutions at the village, intermediate, and district levels, and also for the establishment of Municipalities at the urban level. They also provide for the reservation of seats for SCs and STs and for women.
Good governance refers to the process of governance that is transparent, accountable, participatory, and responsive to the needs of citizens.

questions.q: What are the main recommendations of the Sachar Committee?
questions.answer: The Sachar Committee recommended the establishment of a Minorities Commission, the creation of a National Commission on Minorities, and the implementation of affirmative action policies to promote the welfare of Muslims.

questions.q: Explain the concept of 'Decentralization' in the context of Panchayati Raj Institutions?
questions.answer: Decentralization refers to the transfer of power and authority from the central government to the local governments, in this case, the Panchayati Raj Institutions. It involves the delegation of powers to the local governments to enable them to take decisions and implement programs at the grassroots level.

Participatory governance refers to the process of governance that involves the active participation of citizens in the decision-making process. It involves the creation of institutions and mechanisms that enable citizens to participate in the governance process and hold the government accountable for its actions.

questions.q: Explain the concept of 'Institutional Reforms' in the context of Panchayati Raj Institutions?
questions.answer: Institutional reforms refer to the changes made in the structure and functioning of Panchayati Raj Institutions to make them more effective and responsive to the needs of citizens. This includes changes in the laws, rules, and procedures governing the working of these institutions.
